title: Midpoint Calculator – Midpoint of Two Points
shortDescription: Find the midpoint between two points on the coordinate plane.
description: Enter two points (x₁,y₁) and (x₂,y₂) to get the midpoint of the segment. Runs in your browser — no signup.
metaDescription: Free midpoint calculator. Enter two points to find the midpoint of a line segment using the midpoint formula ((x₁+x₂)/2, (y₁+y₂)/2), instantly in your browser.

result.label: Midpoint (x, y)
result.empty: Enter two points to find the midpoint.

sections.heading: The midpoint formula
paragraphs: The midpoint of a segment is the point exactly halfway between its endpoints. It is found by averaging the coordinates: M = ((x₁ + x₂) / 2, (y₁ + y₂) / 2). The midpoint always lies on the segment and divides it into two equal halves.

sections.heading: Where the midpoint is used
paragraphs: The midpoint is used in geometry to bisect segments, find the center of a circle from a diameter, and locate the centroid of simple shapes. In coordinate geometry it pairs naturally with the slope and distance formulas for the same two points.

faq.a: M = ((x₁ + x₂) / 2, (y₁ + y₂) / 2) — the average of the x-values and the average of the y-values.

faq.q: Is the midpoint always on the segment?
faq.a: Yes. It lies exactly halfway between the two endpoints and divides the segment into equal halves.
Yes. The formula works with negative numbers, decimals, and zero.

faq.q: How is midpoint different from distance?
faq.a: The midpoint is the center point; the distance is the length between the two points.

faq.q: Does order of the points matter?
faq.a: No. Swapping the two points gives the same midpoint.
